title: Add 3-option List/Rent/Invest interface on Home page

requirements.summary: Update HomePage to prominently display a responsive, above-the-fold 3-option interface labeled exactly “List”, “Rent”, and “Invest”, consistent with existing Tailwind typography/spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: When visiting "/", users can clearly see three distinct options labeled "List", "Rent", and "Invest" without scrolling on common laptop/mobile viewports (responsive).
The option labels are in English and match exactly: "List", "Rent", "Invest".
The section is visually consistent with the existing site styling (Tailwind + existing typography/spacing conventions already used on HomePage).

file_operations.description: Within the hero area (above the current fold), add a 3-option UI presenting exactly three primary actions labeled “List”, “Rent”, and “Invest”. Compose existing shadcn-ui primitives already used on the page (e.g., Button) and Tailwind utility classes to match existing HomePage styling; do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui. Ensure responsive layout (e.g., stacked on small screens, 3-column on larger screens) so the three options are visible without scrolling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Wire the HomePage “List”, “Rent”, and “Invest” options to navigate to existing routes using TanStack Router’s useNavigate pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking/tapping "List" navigates to the existing Listing marketing page at "/listing".
Clicking/tapping "Rent" navigates to the existing Rent page at "/rent".
Clicking/tapping "Invest" navigates to the existing Invest page at "/invest".
Navigation uses existing routing patterns already present in HomePage (useNavigate with navigate({ to: ... })).

file_operations.description: Add click/tap handlers for the three options using the existing useNavigate hook, calling navigate({ to: '/listing' }), navigate({ to: '/rent' }), and navigate({ to: '/invest' }) respectively. Keep routing unchanged elsewhere (no App.tsx route edits) and reuse existing HomePage navigation patterns. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
